question.text: An education fee of Rs. 2,00,000 is payable in USD after 6 months. USDINR spot is 82, 6-month premium is 2%, and the person earns 6% p.a. on domestic fixed income. If the person invests Rs. 2,00,000 and hedges with futures, how many USD can be remitted?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Investment maturity: Rs. 2,00,000 + interest (6% × 6/12 = Rs. 6,000) = Rs. 2,06,000. 6-month futures rate = 82 × (1 + 2%) = Rs. 83.64. USD available = 2,06,000 / 83.64 = USD 2,462.94. **The Trap:** Using spot rate of 82 instead of futures rate gives USD 2,512.20 — but the hedger locks in the futures rate, not the spot rate. USD 2,500 ignores the interest earned on the investment. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I Ch5 covers this exact education fee hedging example. Series XIII tests whether students can combine investment returns with currency hedging. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For overseas remittances, always factor in: (1) domestic return on the invested amount, and (2) the forward premium reflected in the futures price.
